steps.title: Let the starting number be N
steps.content: Let the initial number of prey and predators each be $N$.

steps.title: Compute the final counts
steps.content:
Prey increased by 2700% of $N$, so the increase is $27N$ and the final prey count is $N+27N = 28N$.
Predators increased by 180% of $N$, so the increase is $1.8N$ and the final predator count is $N+1.8N = 2.8N$.

steps.title: Find the percent by which prey exceeds predators
steps.content:
The ratio of final prey to final predators is $\dfrac{28N}{2.8N}=10$.
If prey is $p\%$ greater than predators, then $\dfrac{\text{prey}}{\text{predators}} = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$.
Thus $1 + \frac{p}{100}=10$, giving $\frac{p}{100}=9$ and $p=900$.
The answer is 900.
